description_long: This rifle features a robust wooden stock with a dark, polished finish, seamlessly connected to a metal receiver and barrel assembly. The barrel is long and cylindrical, with a series of vent holes along the upper handguard, suggesting a design for heat dissipation. A prominent drum magazine is mounted beneath the receiver, indicating a high-capacity feeding system. The rifle is equipped with simple iron sights, with the front sight post and rear notch clearly visible. The overall construction combines wood and metal, giving it a classic, utilitarian appearance typical of mid-20th-century military firearms. The finish is matte with some glossy highlights on the metal parts, and the object appears well-crafted and solid.
description: A classic rifle with a wooden stock, metal barrel, and a large drum magazine, featuring iron sights and a robust, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: The rifle is seen from the front, showing the circular drum magazine and the front sight post aligned with the barrel.
description_view_1: A full left-side profile reveals the wooden stock, long barrel with vent holes, drum magazine, and iron sights.
description_view_2: The rear view highlights the end of the stock and the back of the drum magazine, with the rear sight visible.
description_view_3: A full right-side profile similar to view 1, showing the wooden stock, metal barrel, drum magazine, and iron sights from the opposite side.
